paper_title: General procedure of building the GLOF inventory

methods.description: A six-step procedure to build a GLOF inventory by integrating literature review, remote sensing imagery analysis, and field validation.

steps.description: We reviewed existing literature (including searches in Web of Science and Scopus databases, a grey literature search and a documentary data search; see Section 3.2), returning 53 GLOFs, which we placed into the inventory.
steps.searchable_summary: search and compile GLOF data from scientific and grey literature

steps.description: We visually explored remote sensing imagery and created a dataset of potential GLOF sites, based on the mapping of GLOF diagnostic features.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze remote sensing imagery to identify potential GLOF sites

steps.description: Additional mapping of GLOF diagnostic features was undertaken by a parallel group of team members; this largely confirmed the mapping results from Step 2, although an additional six GLOFs were included at this stage.
steps.searchable_summary: validate and confirm GLOF site mapping with additional team analysis

steps.description: Quantitative characteristics, including longitude, latitude and elevation of GLOF-producing lakes, were derived automatically in a GIS environment (QGIS, v. 3.10 A Coruña).
steps.searchable_summary: derive quantitative characteristics of GLOF sites using GIS

steps.description: Qualitative characteristics (dam type, drainage type, mechanism of water release, event type, lake-glacier interaction at the time of GLOF) were assigned manually based on the visual interpretation of high-resolution remotely sensed imagery.
steps.searchable_summary: assign qualitative characteristics to GLOF sites using imagery interpretation

steps.description: GLOF timing was established, in many cases, from existing literature and documentary data. For cases where this was not possible, remotely sensed images were employed to establish approximate GLOF timing.
steps.searchable_summary: determine GLOF timing using literature and remote sensing
